Class User

java.lang.Object
com.linecorp.centraldogma.server.metadata.User
All Implemented Interfaces:
Identifiable, Serializable
Direct Known Subclasses:
UserWithAppIdentity
public class User extends Object implements Identifiable, Serializable
A user.

  • Constructor Details

    • User

      public User(String login, String name, String email, List<String> roles)
      Creates a new instance.

    • User

      public User(String name, String email)
      Creates a new instance.

    • User

      public User(String login)
      Creates a new instance.

    • User

      public User(String login, List<String> roles)
      Creates a new instance from a login string.

      This constructor handles two cases for the provided login:

      • If the login is a valid email address, the name() is extracted from the local part of the email.
      • If the login is not a valid email address, it is treated as a username. The Util.USER_EMAIL_SUFFIX is appended to create a valid email address.

      Examples: 

      // Example 1: Login is a valid email address
User user1 = new User("john.doe@example.com", ...);
// user1.email() will be "john.doe@example.com"
// user1.name() will be "john.doe"

// Example 2: Login is not an email address
User user2 = new User("jane.doe", ...);
// user2.email() will be "jane.doe@localhost.localdomain"
// user2.name() will be "jane.doe"
      Parameters:
      login - the user's login string, which can be a username or a full email address.

  • Method Details
